commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Telegram silent-marker helper, normalizes trailing standalone `notify=false` in durable outbound and native reply delivery, suppresses marker-only sends, and adds Telegram regression tests.

PR surface: Source +93, Tests +149. Total +242 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows Telegram delivery honors structured `silent` metadata but has no current marker parser before durable/native delivery, matching the linked issue's visible marker leak; I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual follow-up is needed because contributor/live Telegram proof remains a merge gate; the code repair is narrow, but automation cannot supply the contributor's real-environment proof.

target.title: fix(telegram): consume trailing silent notify marker
target.body:
Closes #80569

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Telegram replies that ended with a standalone `notify=false` delivery marker would still notify users and leak that marker into the visible Telegram message body.

## Why This Change Was Made

Telegram now consumes only a trailing standalone `notify=false` marker at its delivery boundary, strips it from the sent text, and maps it to the existing silent-delivery option. The marker is normalized before durable text chunking so every Telegram send for that payload inherits `silent`, while inline explanatory text containing `notify=false` remains visible.

Marker-only payloads are suppressed as no-op deliveries instead of fabricating Telegram message identities or attempting empty Telegram sends. The same helper is used by native reply delivery so hook-mutated or native Telegram replies follow the same rule.

## User Impact

Telegram users no longer see `notify=false` as message text when it is used as a trailing delivery-control marker, and silent-intent replies are sent with Telegram silent notification metadata. Normal Telegram text that merely mentions `notify=false` is unchanged.

## Evidence

- Duplicate PR gate before implementation:
  - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body -- "80569"` -> no open PRs.
  - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body -- "notify=false"` -> no direct open fix for this issue.
- Focused Telegram delivery coverage:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts`
  - Result: 2 files passed, 98 tests passed.
- Heartbeat response-tool regression slice:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts -- -t "treats notify=false as a quiet heartbeat ack|delivers notificationText when notify=true"`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 2 tests passed, 14 skipped.
- Formatting and whitespace:
  - `E:\code\openclaw\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/silent-marker.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.
  - `git diff --check` -> clean.
- Autoreview:
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
  - Final result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Not tested: a live Telegram bot/group/topic delivery with real credentials. The proof here exercises the actual Telegram adapter/native delivery send seams with mocked Telegram API calls, including marker stripping, silent option propagation, inline-text preservation, durable long-text pre-chunk normalization, and marker-only no-op handling.
target.author: qingminglong
